meaning: He is exalted, He is full of blessings
intense harm (as the object being acted upon)
he fabricated/invented it (referring to the Quran, which they falsely claimed was made up)
and he helped him (referring to the Quran that was mentioned before)
he wrote it down for himself
a treasure (any treasure, not a specific one)
the people who wrong others (the speakers in this verse)
look! (command to one person) at كَيْفَ (how)
He is supremely blessed (referring to Allah)
he made/set (referring to Allah in this ayah)
better (as a description of what Allah can give)
and we made ready (for those who denied the Hour)
she saw them (referring to the Fire of Hell)
for her/it (referring to Hell in this ayah)
from it/her (referring to the narrow place mentioned after)
a place (that they were thrown into)
there, in that place (referring to the tight/constricted place mentioned)
destruction/ruin (as the object being called for)
surely for them (they have what they want in it)
these ones (referring to false gods being asked about)
or (asking if they misguided the servants or if the servants went astray themselves)
they (the false gods being asked about)
but/and however (connects and contrasts with previous statement)
you indeed provided them much enjoyment
any help (as something they cannot do)
a punishment (that will be given to them)
our Lord (being called upon by those who reject faith)
in great rebellion (describing how they acted)
for the criminals (receiving no glad tidings)
on that day (when the companions of Paradise will be better settled)
a sending down (as a direct object)
and he was (connecting this description to what came before)
difficult (describing the day for the disbelievers)
Oh, woe is me! (expressing deep regret)
by your Lord (used as an oath)
and a supporter/defender (as the object being described)
why not (was the Quran revealed all at once)
like that, in that way (referring to how the Quran was revealed gradually)
so that we may make firm
except (what comes after this word is different from what was mentioned before)
and (He made it) better/more excellent (than their explanation)
Moses (the prophet who received the Torah)
and we made (something into something else)
both of you go! (command to two people)
and Thamud (as object of an implied verb 'We destroyed')
the deep well (referring to a specific ancient well)
that (far) (pointing to the previously mentioned nations)
they see it (referring to the town mentioned in the ayah)
resurrection (as the object being hoped for)
he will surely lead us astray
they know (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
more lost from the right path
his desire (which he took as his god)
a guardian/trustee (being described in this ayah as something they won't be)
they think deeply (referring to those who reject the truth)
except (what follows from what was mentioned before)
Rather (contradicting the previous statement about them being like cattle)
he would surely have made it
he sent (as an active command/caused to be sent)
as good news (describing how Allah sent the winds)
His specific act of kindness and care
so that we give life to
with it/by means of it (referring to water that is used to give life to dead land)
and we give drink to him
we distributed it repeatedly (referring to the rain mentioned earlier)
between them (referring to the people/creatures Allah distributed something among)
so that they may take remembrance
and struggle against them! (command to one person)
and this (pointing to the salty water mentioned right after)
salt (describing the salty water in the verse)
and he (the one who created from water)
a helper/supporter (for the disbeliever against Allah)
we sent you (as a messenger to people)
his Lord (referring to whoever chooses to take a path to Him)
The Extremely Merciful One (one of Allah's names)
they said (responding to the command to prostrate)
should we bow down in worship? (questioning tone)
they walk (referring to the servants of the Most Merciful)
they said (referring to the servants of the Most Merciful mentioned earlier)
to their lord (prostrating and standing to)
prostrating (describing their state while spending the night in worship)
and in a standing position (describing how they spend the night)
indeed it (referring to Hell mentioned before)
a balanced way (as the way they spend in this ayah)
a penalty for sin (that will be encountered)
except (introduces an exception to what was mentioned before)
Allah (the one true God who changes their bad deeds to good deeds)
constantly forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
one who shows constant mercy (in object form)
then indeed he (the one who repents)
upon it/them (referring to the signs/verses of their Lord mentioned earlier)
they are rewarded (referring to the dwellers of Paradise)
and peace (as part of a greeting)
it (Paradise) has become beautiful (as a resting place)
my Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for me)
if not, were it not for
